params.negative_prompt: no text, no words, no letters, no numbers, no digits, no labels, no stickers, no decals, no placards, no serial tags, no serial plates, no engraved plates, no warning signs, no warning plates, no stamped markings, no screen digits, no icon plates, no symbol plates, no interface readouts, no readable ui, no fireball, no ignition plume, no launch already in progress, no explosion imagery, no second tower as hero, no roadway as subject, no poster composition, no multi-object launch complex, no literal stained-glass window, no kaleidoscope, no rainbow fog, no smeared lens flare, no glossy sci-fi pad
params.note_text: Optical-glass pilot for Challenger beat 01. Preserve the frozen pad warning as documentary hardware seen through premium refractive cold-glass atmosphere.
params.accent_palette: glacial white, smoked-glass blue, oxidized teal, prism amber
params.safe_zone_intent: keep the frozen gantry evidence and any witness trace above the subtitle band and preserve the top UI band as cold empty air
params.signal_anchor: documented prelaunch warning held in frozen gantry steel and pad ice, refracted through a premium cold-glass atmosphere before Challenger flies
params.historical_anchor: Roger Boisjoly's warning held against the frozen Challenger launch pad before liftoff
params.surreal_breach: thin optical refractions in the icy air make the warning feel physically trapped in the launch hardware without turning the scene into spectacle

prompt_fragments.anchor_fragment: frozen gantry steel, pad ice, and one implied human warning compressed into a single prelaunch evidence frame seen through refined optical glass
support_fragments: documentary launch hardware preserved beneath cold refractive air and smoked-glass tonal separation
premium liquid-glass edge splits and icy vapor held subordinate to the warning atmosphere
directives: make the optical-glass effect intrinsic to the image, not a separate overlay
keep the frame documentary and prelaunch, never explosive
avoid kaleidoscope, rainbow fog, or sci-fi lens gimmicks
draft_directives: keep the launch environment above the subtitle band
no fireball, blast plume, or already-launched shuttle
no collage panels or pasted source fragments
